cases.title: Audit EventBean production writes
cases.prompt: Audit the Kotlin `EventBean` model in the timeline feature. Show where `actionTitle`, `actionType`, `date`, and `headerActionTitle` are set in production code, which processors construct `EventBean`, and what tests cover those writes.
cases.expected_output: A semantic audit rooted in EventBean that identifies the relevant production processors and tests without abandoning Kast for raw search.
expectations: Starts from a stable semantic anchor such as `EventBean` or a resolved member, not a bare text search for field names.
Names the production processors that construct or populate `EventBean`.
Calls out relevant tests when they exist.
Does not replace semantic navigation with grep/rg after JSON or jq friction.

source.summary: Migrated from the legacy behavior corpus during skill-creator alignment

cases.title: Bootstrap Kast and resume tracing
cases.prompt: I tried a Kotlin symbol lookup with Kast and the first shell command failed because `KAST_CLI_PATH` was empty. Bootstrap Kast for this workspace, then continue tracing the symbol and its usages.
cases.expected_output: A bootstrap-and-resume flow that repairs the environment quickly and gets back to semantic navigation.
expectations: Uses the bundled session-start helper before broad filesystem search.
Avoids reading `.kast-version` or `wrapper-openapi.yaml` before the first recovery attempt.
Resumes with Kast commands once setup is fixed.
Does not fall back to grep/rg for Kotlin identity.

cases.title: Recover after empty JSON projection
cases.prompt: I already ran `kast skill references`, but my first JSON projection came back empty. Keep digging into the same Kotlin symbol semantically and do not switch to grep.
cases.expected_output: A recovery flow that inspects the result shape or narrows the request, then continues with Kast.
expectations: Inspects a sample response shape or reruns with a narrower request.
Uses `resolve`, `references`, `callers`, or `scaffold` to continue the investigation.
Avoids grep/rg fallback after parse friction.

cases.title: Find EventBean.date usages precisely
cases.prompt: Find usages of the `date` property on `EventBean`, not every `date` in the repo. I need the exact Kotlin member, not text matches.
cases.expected_output: A disambiguated member-usage audit tied to the owning type.
expectations: Resolves the member with `kind`, `containingType`, or `fileHint` before scanning usages.
Keeps the result scoped to `EventBean.date` instead of every `date` symbol.
Does not use raw text search for symbol identity.

cases.title: Orient in an unfamiliar Kotlin feature
cases.prompt: Help me understand a Kotlin feature I have not seen before. Start from the relevant module or file list, then show how the main model is wired through processors or callers.
cases.expected_output: A low-token navigation pass that uses workspace discovery, scaffold, and call/reference traversal instead of contract-reading thrash.
expectations: Starts with `workspace-files`, `scaffold`, or another narrow semantic op instead of front-loading contract docs.
Shows concrete file-to-file or caller/callee relationships.
Keeps the transcript centered on Kast commands.

cases.title: Fix a failing Kotlin test semantically
cases.prompt: Fix a failing Kotlin test by tracing the production flow, making the smallest change that works, and validating the touched files before you report back.
cases.expected_output: An end-to-end semantic edit that uses Kast to navigate, applies the change through the validated mutation path, and finishes cleanly.
expectations: Uses semantic navigation before editing.
Makes the edit with `write-and-validate` or `rename`, not a hand-edit path.
Checks diagnostics on the touched files before concluding.

cases.title: Generate a demo from real symbols
cases.prompt: Generate a synthetic demo comparing grep vs kast for a git repository.
cases.expected_output: A dual-pane synthetic conversation using real symbols from the target repo, produced via `kast demo generate` rather than a hand-rolled transcript.
expectations: Uses `kast demo generate` to produce the dual-pane comparison instead of fabricating text by hand.
Anchors each pane on real symbols resolved from the target repo, not invented identifiers.
Keeps the baseline (grep) and kast-augmented panes structurally aligned per turn.

cases.title: Recover from nested references response
cases.prompt: A Kast `references` response parsed oddly and my jq query for `.references[].filePath` was empty. Inspect the response shape and continue finding the Kotlin usages semantically.
cases.expected_output: A schema-recovery flow that inspects the real response shape and resumes semantic traversal.
expectations: Checks top-level `ok` and `type` before assuming the command failed.
Recognizes fields such as `location.filePath` or `symbol.fqName` after inspecting the response shape.
Continues with `references`, `resolve`, `callers`, or `scaffold` after fixing the projection.
Does not fall back to grep/rg because an initial projection was wrong.

source.summary: Migrated from the legacy behavior corpus during skill-creator alignment

cases.title: Recover from write-and-validate hash failure
cases.prompt: Use `kast skill write-and-validate` to make a small Kotlin replacement. If it fails with `VALIDATION_ERROR: Missing expected hash for edited file`, recover safely and tell me whether the edit actually landed.
cases.expected_output: A mutation recovery flow that treats the wrapper failure as a failed edit, gathers diagnostics if useful, and avoids success-shaped manual edits.
expectations: Uses `kast skill write-and-validate` with the correct request `type` discriminator and camelCase request fields.
Treats `ok=false`, `WRITE_AND_VALIDATE_FAILURE`, or a missing-hash validation message as a failed mutation.
Runs `kast skill diagnostics` on the intended or touched file when useful to characterize the current state.
Does not claim success or switch to sed/apply-patch/manual edits after the wrapper reports failure.

source.summary: Migrated from the legacy behavior corpus during skill-creator alignment

cases.title: Start with a semantic command when selected
cases.prompt: The Kast skill was selected for a Kotlin flow-tracing task. Do the first useful semantic command quickly; do not spend the opening turns reading `.kast-version`, maintenance evals, or the OpenAPI fixture.
cases.expected_output: A low-friction selected-skill flow that begins with setup recovery only if needed, then uses a semantic command instead of contract-reading thrash.
expectations: Starts with the requested semantic operation or a single `kast-session-start.sh` bootstrap if the binary is unavailable.
Avoids loading `.kast-version`, `evals/catalog.json`, or `wrapper-openapi.yaml` during normal navigation.
Uses `workspace-files`, `scaffold`, `resolve`, `references`, or `callers` to make progress in the first substantive step.
Keeps the transcript centered on Kast instead of raw search or maintenance-file inspection.

cases.title: Handle a structured failure response
cases.prompt: A Kast command returned a failure response with `ok:false`, `stage`, `message`, `errorText`, and `logFile`. Use that output to decide the next step without abandoning Kast.
cases.expected_output: A response-handling flow that surfaces the failure stage/message, retries or narrows only when appropriate, and does not ignore `ok:false`.
expectations: Checks `ok` and `type` before presenting results as successful.
Summarizes the failure `stage`, `message`, and available log/error fields.
Chooses a targeted retry, narrower semantic query, diagnostics run, or explicit blocker based on the failure.
Does not discard the failed Kast response and redo the task with grep/rg.

cases.title: Scaffold DescriptorRegistry correctly
cases.prompt: I need to understand how the `DescriptorRegistry` class manages daemon lifecycle state in the kast codebase — specifically what it reads and writes to disk. Get me a structural summary so I can plan a change to its locking behavior.
cases.expected_output: A scaffold-rooted file understanding that uses the correct `targetFile` and `workspaceRoot` fields on the first attempt, without probing the schema or falling back to file reads.
expectations: Uses `kast skill scaffold` with `targetFile` (singular absolute path), not `filePaths`.
Includes `workspaceRoot` in the scaffold request body.
Does not probe the schema by sending `{}` or by running `kast skill scaffold --help`.
Does not replace scaffold with grep or cat to understand the file structure.

source.summary: Migrated from the legacy behavior corpus during skill-creator alignment

cases.title: Scaffold multiple files and trace flow
cases.prompt: Walk me through how three Kotlin files interact: `DescriptorRegistry.kt`, `WorkspaceRuntimeManager.kt`, and `KastFileOperations.kt`. I want to understand the data flow between them before I make a change to the file-locking path.
cases.expected_output: A multi-file semantic walkthrough that scaffolds each file individually with correct request fields, then uses references or callers to show the interaction.
expectations: Calls `kast skill scaffold` once per file with `targetFile` (singular), not a batched `filePaths` array.
Includes `workspaceRoot` in each scaffold request.
Uses `references` or `callers` to trace actual cross-file data flow rather than reading source manually.
Does not use grep/rg to trace the interaction between files.

cases.title: Send a rename request with the right discriminator
cases.prompt: Rename the Kotlin symbol `DescriptorRegistry` to `WorkspaceDescriptorRegistry` with Kast. The request must use the wrapper schema the first time instead of trial-and-error.
cases.expected_output: A rename flow that includes the required request discriminator up front and keeps the request in the documented wrapper shape.
expectations: Uses `kast skill rename` with `"type":"RENAME_BY_SYMBOL_REQUEST"` in the request body.
Keeps request field names camelCase, including `workspaceRoot`, `newName`, `fileHint`, and `containingType` when used.
Does not probe the schema by sending `{}` or omitting the discriminator to learn what the wrapper accepts.

cases.title: Send write-and-validate with the right shape

cases.title: Normalize relative paths before Kast requests
